_meta.methodology: BUILD_PROCESS.md (14-param + principles + functional/security/concurrency tests); P0.7 zero-debt

title: Lead Import (CR-C): duplicate review + merge (possible_duplicate -> resolve/merge) + convert block on confirmed duplicate
business_reason: CR-A/B skip duplicates and only report them. duplicate_policy defines a review lifecycle (unchecked/possible_duplicate/confirmed_duplicate/not_duplicate/merged/ignored) with merge_allowed + merge_requires_reason + merged_lead_preserves_history, and auto_block_conversion_when_confirmed_duplicate. CR-C surfaces import collisions for review and lets staff resolve or merge them instead of silently dropping data.

drift_findings_step3.columns: school_leads.duplicate_status enum [unchecked,possible_duplicate,confirmed_duplicate,not_duplicate,merged,ignored]; duplicate_of_lead_id uuid; lead_status includes duplicate_review + archived.
drift_findings_step3.convert: convertLead is a chain action; add a precondition blocking when duplicate_status = confirmed_duplicate (auto_block_conversion_when_confirmed_duplicate).

param_14.question: Surface import duplicates for review and let staff resolve or merge them; block converting a confirmed duplicate.
param_14.answer: Import: collisions are STAGED + inserted as duplicate_status=possible_duplicate, lead_status=duplicate_review, duplicate_of_lead_id=matched existing lead (when known) — no longer silently skipped. resolve_duplicate(status): not_duplicate -> active/clear pointer; confirmed_duplicate -> stays (blocks convert); ignored. merge_duplicate(target_lead_id, reason): re-parent this lead's interactions to the target (merged_lead_preserves_history), set duplicate_status=merged + lead_status=archived + archived_at + duplicate_of_lead_id=target; reason required + audited. convertLead precondition: block when duplicate_status=confirmed_duplicate.
param_14.security_implications: resolve/merge are staff-write + assertLeadInScope (FR-0006 carries through). merge requires a reason (P1.8) and is audited with the original pointer (P3.5). Re-parenting only moves interactions within the same scope.
param_14.approval_implications: none (review is single-actor); confirmed_duplicate gates conversion (P3.11)
param_14.downstream_dependencies: extends dedupe to return the matched existing id (classifyImport); convert chain gains a precondition mechanism (reusable)
param_14.assumptions: Within-batch duplicates are flagged possible_duplicate with a null pointer (no id yet). Merge target must be a real lead id; merging is not reversible (archived).

principles_pass.surface_not_skip: duplicate_policy — import collisions become reviewable possible_duplicate rows, not silent drops
principles_pass.merge_reason: P1.8/P3.5 — merge requires a reason; audited; history preserved (interactions re-parented, not deleted)
principles_pass.enums: P2.2 — duplicate_status set from the canonical enum; resolve/merge validate the target status
principles_pass.reuse: P0.6 — convert precondition is a generic chain mechanism; resolve uses the row-action generator

functional: import collision inserts a possible_duplicate (duplicate_review) row with duplicate_of pointer
convert blocked when confirmed_duplicate; allowed otherwise
security_owasp: A01: resolve/merge on an out-of-scope lead rejected (assertLeadInScope)
A03: resolve rejects an out-of-enum status
A09: merge audited with reason + target
concurrency: two merges of the same lead -> one applies; both audited
resolve + convert race: convert sees confirmed_duplicate and blocks
